repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University Department of Physics records
did.physdesc: Correspondence, biographical material, clippings, photographs, notes, diagrams, publications, monographs, notebooks, photographs, account books, inventory books, personnel records, glass slides and other items.
did.abstract: Correspondence, biographical material, clippings, photographs, notes, diagrams, publications, glass slides and other items pertaining to the Cornell University Department of Physics, primarily from the 1880s through the 1950s, along with some later memoirs and histories of the Department and those associated with it.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, biographical material, clippings, photographs, notes, diagrams, publications, and other items pertaining to Cornell physics professors William A. Anthony, Frederick Bedell, Ernest G. Merritt, George S. Moler, Edward L. Nichols, and Floyd K. Richtmyer, as well as physicist Ernest Fox Nichols (Cornell University M.S. 1892); the correspondence pertains to efforts to obtain the biographical material, departmental administration, the study of physics, and other professional matters, such as the dynamo built by Anthony and Moler ca.1875, and its exhibition at the Century of Progress International Exposition in Chicago, 1933, the establishment of the date Bedell invented his stabilized oscilloscope, and negotiations, 1926, for bringing the German physicist, Max Born, to Cornell. Includes correspondence of Livingston Farrand and John Henry Comstock. Also, physics notebooks, 1904-1931, with research data of Edward L. Nichols, Horace L. Howes, Ernest G. Merritt, D. T. Wilber, and others on phosphorescence, fluorescence, luminescence, and spectra, scientific photographs and charts, undated typescripts on various subjects by Nichols, Howes, and Wilber, and a monograph, apparently by Nichols and his associates, on the fluorescence and afterglow of solid solutions and their luminescence when rendered incandescent by a hydrogen flame, as well as accounts, 1913-1932, pertaining to a Carnegie Institution grant for this research.

In addition, indexed department account books, 1882-1948; inventory books, 1881-1933; and a "Committee Report on Survey of Floor Area Requirements for Physics, Engineering Physics, and Astronomy for a 15-Year Period, 1947-1962," ca.1946; and personnel records of former faculty, staff, and graduate students who were appointed to the Physics Dept. prior to 1935. Manuscript entitled "Seventy Years of Physics at Cornell," by Harley E. Howe and Guy E. Grantham, 1958; bound typescript, "The Cornell Physics Department," by Paul Hartman, 1982, with an index; and chart of department staff, 1868-1940; and The Cornell Physics Department: Recollections and a History of Sorts, by Paul Hartman, 1984. Administrative correspondence of the department, primarily of George S. Moler, 1893-1900. Also, Applied and Engineering Physics at Cornell, and A Memoir on the Physical Review (mentioning Cornell connections) by Paul Hartman.
Includes files from the 1950s, mostly of Carl Gartlein; blueprints, photographs, subject files, departmental correspondence, and other papers relating to the Physics Department.

Also a timeline of Physics Faculty and Staff 1867-1955.
Glass slides from 1917-1918, 1926, 1861 and 1875. Early glass slides come from Professor Edward Leamington Nichols'cources and research, covering topics such as Experimental Physics, Uranyl and Sulfone fluorescein. Later slides cover topics such as Uranyl Ammonium Chloride, Small Particles Ni, 1 and Fe, 1, as well as observation notes by date and photographs on Sun Trial, Pt.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Department of Physics records, #14-22-693. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to Boxes 25-31 restricted to the permission of the Cornell University Department of Physics.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide. Also contains a time-line of Physics Faculty and Staff 1867-1955.

did.unittitle: Davey, Mrs. W. P. was Laura Gunn
Goldsmith, Mrs. N.W. was Leah WELLS
Meacham, Edna L. - Mrs. L.A. Welo
Shapiro, Casper V. - Casper, Casper V.
Shaw, Mrs. R. W. Charlotte Throop
Slattery, Mabel K. - Mrs. G.P. Vincent
Snow, Mrs. Olive B. (now Mrs. Daley)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marrit Scott was an assistant in Physics. Some of the slides are broken.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sarah Alice McNulty, A.B., was an Assistant in Physics and Chemistry between 1922-1923.

did.unittitle: Introductory Experimental Physics course. Professor Edward Leamington Nichols and Research assistant, Roswell Clifton Gibbs (R.C.J.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten note: These Slides were loaned to Mr. Simon Fred at Columbia University and were returned after a few months. R.C. J. 50 Lantern Slide Plates, 4x3.1/4

did.unittitle: Uranyl Negatives, Professor Edward Leamington Nichols

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten note: 6 Plates, E.L.N. Eastman Kodak Co. 20 Lantern slide plates, Seed dry plates 4x3.1/4.

did.unittitle: Uranyl Spectra Negatives, by Professor Edward Leamington Nichols
Sulfone fluorescein, Professor Edward Leamington Nichols

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eastman Kodak Co. 6 Seed Dry Plates. Eastman Kodak Co., 4x3.1/4.

did.unittitle: Uranyl negatives, Professor Edward Leamington Nichols

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eastman Kodak Co. 6 Seed Dry Plates. Eastman Kodak Co., 4x3.1/4.

did.unittitle: Lantern Slide Plates, by Professor Edward Leamington Nichols

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Troy, Cornell University Photographer. The Imperial Dry Plate Co. Ltd. Cricklewood, London N.W. 7 Lantern Slide Plates, 4x3.1/4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten note: Absorption Speech of: Uranyl Acetate, Barium Uranyl Acetate, Rubidium Uranyl Sulphone, Uranyl Ammonium Chlorides. A.L Hook. 13 Glass Slides, 4x10. Eastman Kodak Co.

did.unittitle: Observation notes by date and photographs on Sun Trial, Pt.
